description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: city in Santa Cruz County, California, United States
description_wiki: Watsonville is a city in Santa Cruz County, California, in the Monterey Bay Area of the Central Coast of California.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.
John Lesesne DeWitt (9 January 1880 – 20 June 1962) was a four-star general in the United States Army.

description: 1942 U.S. presidential order for the internment of Japanese-Americans
description_wiki: Executive Order 9066 was a United States presidential executive order signed and issued during World War II by United States president Franklin D. Roosevelt on February 19, 1942.

description: city in Yolo County, California, United States
description_wiki: Davis is the most populous city in Yolo County, California, United States.

The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

description: World War II internment camp for Japanese-Americans
description_wiki: The Rohwer War Relocation Center was a World War II Japanese American concentration camp located in rural southeastern Arkansas, in Desha County.

description: World War II Japanese-American internment camp in California
Internment camp for Japanese Americans in Millard County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: The Topaz War Relocation Center, also known as the Central Utah Relocation Center (Topaz) and briefly as the Abraham Relocation Center, was an American concentration camp in which Americans of Japanese descent and immigrants who had come to the United States from Japan, called Nikkei were incarcerated.

description: US government agency established to handle the internment of Japanese Americans during World War II
description_wiki: The War Relocation Authority (WRA) was a United States government agency established to handle the internment of Japanese Americans during World War II.  It also operated the Fort Ontario Emergency Refugee Shelter in Oswego, New York, which was the only refugee camp set up in the United States for refugees from Europe.

description: city in and county seat of Cuyahoga County, Ohio, United States
description_wiki: Cleveland ( KLEEV-lənd) is a city in the U.S. state of Ohio and the county seat of Cuyahoga County.
